title: Safety-Relevant Identity Absorption Testbed (nationality + religion + ethnicity-identity + named-entity) — data_out.json
description: On-disk format is the AII `exp_sel_data_out` schema: a top-level object {metadata, datasets:[{dataset, examples}]}. Each example carries input, output (the PARENT binary label), and FLAT metadata_<key> fields (the exp_sel schema forbids nested objects inside an example). The 'logical_row_schema' block documents the equivalent nested {input, output, metadata{...}} view. This is a strict structural drop-in for the iter-1 non-spelling/taxonomic absorption testbed (gen_art_dataset_2) and the iter-5 homograph testbed: same on-disk format, same three components per hierarchy (content-flip pairs / surface-flip pairs / diagnostic corpus), same fold semantics, same gemma-2-2b token anchoring, same pinned pile revision. The four hierarchies transpose the Georgia-homograph absorption structure onto SAFETY-RELEVANT identity attributes (nationality, religion, race/ethnicity, public-figure/organization). Sub-context / target-sense / competitor labels are assigned PURELY from surface form / gazetteer / high-precision disambiguating local context, independent of any SAE latent or model — so the degenerate-construction guard holds and absorption presence/absence is an iteration-6+ empirical finding, NOT baked into the data. NEXT-ITERATION (M2') building block — NOT consumed by this iteration's parallel experiments.

metadata.description: Top-level build metadata (seed, pile revision, civil-supplement flag, counts, llm cost, eligible-entity counts, ...).

input.description: Text the model reads: a content/surface pair sentence, or a pile/civil context window.

output.description: PARENT binary label: positive = the parent identity concept (is-a-nationality / is-a-religion / is-a-ethnicity-identity / is-a-named-entity) is present at the target token; negative = absent.

metadata_parent_concept.description: Plain-words name of the general parent concept for this hierarchy (e.g. 'a nationality / demonym ...').

metadata_sub_context.description: Frozen surface-derived sub-context = the canonical identity token (e.g. Polish, Muslim, Black, Apple) for positives; null for negatives / x_off.

metadata_pair_id.description: Links x_on<->x_off and surface_a<->surface_b; null for corpus rows.

metadata_target_text.description: Exact surface token(s) inside input ('' for x_off where the concept is absent). For homograph_distractor negatives this is the competing-sense token.

metadata_target_char_start.description: Char offset of target in input (-1 if none; for x_off = the differing slot start).

metadata_target_token_indices.description: gemma-2-2b token indices overlapping the char span (offset-mapping, add_special_tokens=False). null if tokenizer unavailable / no span.

metadata_multi_token.description: True iff the target spans more than one gemma-2-2b token (e.g. multi-token demonyms / 'African American').

metadata_pile_set_name.description: Pile sub-corpus name for pile_uncopyrighted rows (e.g. Pile-CC, Wikipedia (en)); null otherwise.

metadata_llm_judge_pass.description: content_flipped AND surface_preserved AND grammatical AND sense_correct. null if not judged. Use pass==true for the primary fold.

metadata_fold.description: pairs -> train/test (by pair_id, stratified by sub_context); corpus -> train/diagnostic (stratified). The diagnostic fold is where the form-free parent-hole / absorption search runs.

metadata_neg_family.description: non_identity = content x_off filler (a surface-matched NON-identity word). other_group = content x_off filler that IS an identity word but of a DIFFERENT parent (parent-specificity control). homograph_distractor = corpus negative: the SAME identity surface in its competing NON-identity sense (the matched hard control). easy = corpus window with no identity token.

metadata_entity.description: Canonical identity token (e.g. Polish, Muslim, Black, Apple). Set on positives and on homograph_distractor negatives (records which token's competing sense appeared); null on x_off / other_group filler / easy.

metadata_target_sense.description: The hierarchy name for target-sense positives; 'competitor' for homograph_distractor negatives; null otherwise.

metadata_homograph_sense.description: True iff this identity token carries a dominant competing NON-identity sense (e.g. Black/White colour, Polish 'to polish', Apple the fruit) — the most likely to be absorption-structured.

metadata_dominant_other_sense.description: Plain-words gloss of the competing (non-identity) sense, e.g. 'the colour black / dark' for Black.

metadata_homograph_strength.description: wordfreq Zipf frequency of the lowercase competing common word (higher = stronger competing sense). null where no homograph / no competing word.

metadata_safety_relevant.description: Always true — every row of this testbed targets a safety-relevant identity attribute.

metadata_identity_label_source.description: Corroborating identity/toxicity flag from a supplement dataset (e.g. 'civil_comments:toxicity>=0.5'); null for pile/templated rows. The sub_context label is ALWAYS surface-derived; this is corroboration only.

logical_row_schema._comment: Equivalent nested view. On disk these metadata_* keys are flattened to satisfy exp_sel_data_out.

metadata.target_sense: nationality | religion | ethnicity_identity | named_entity_safety | competitor | null

cross_hierarchy_collisions: Jewish (religion AND ethnicity; canonical owner = ethnicity_identity)
Indian (India-nationality vs Native-American vs Indian Ocean)
